```json
  "findings": [
    "Explosionproof or classified-area sealing fitting has an unpoured seal with visible fill hole plug still in place.",
    "The seal compound has not been installed, leaving the classified boundary open and the wiring method non-compliant.",
    "Unsealed conduit in a hazardous (classified) location can transmit gases, vapors, or flames between enclosures or into non-classified areas."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the fill hole plug and pour the approved sealing compound per the fitting manufacturer's instructions and project specifications.",
    "Verify the correct sealant type, pour depth, and curing time for the classified location and ambient conditions.",
    "Reinstall the fill plug after the seal has properly cured and inspect for completeness before close-out.",
    "Have a qualified inspector witness and accept the seal before the work is concealed or energized."
  ],
  "rationale": "In hazardous (classified) locations, sealing fittings are required to be filled with an approved sealing compound to prevent the propagation of flammable gases, vapors, or flames through the conduit system. An unpoured seal with the fill plug still visible is a clear non-compliance and creates a serious ignition hazard in a classified area. Because the work is rough-complete, the seal must be completed and inspected before the system is accepted or energized.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 500-516 (Hazardous (Classified) Locations, Sealing and Drainage)"
  ]
}
```
